name: Philips Hue API (CLIP v2)
what_it_does: Local REST API for controlling Philips Hue smart lighting (bulbs, groups, scenes, routines) via the Hue Bridge, with a cloud API for remote access and developer applications.
use_cases: Automating lighting scenes based on time of day, calendar events, or agent triggers
Controlling ambient light for video call optimization or focus mode agents
Triggering visual alerts (light color/flash) for notification agents when events occur
Syncing lighting with music, video content, or game states
Creating presence-aware smart home routines triggered by agent context
not_for: Non-Hue smart home devices (use Home Assistant or Matter for cross-brand control)
Large commercial lighting installations (use Hue for Enterprise or DALI systems)
Sub-100ms real-time lighting control requiring no network latency
best_when: An agent controls smart home lighting specifically for Philips Hue ecosystems in residential or small office settings.
avoid_when: You need cross-brand smart home control or non-Hue hardware compatibility.

auth.notes: Local API: username (API key) obtained via physical button press on Hue Bridge. CLIP v2 uses TLS with bridge certificate. Remote API: OAuth 2.0 via Hue developer account for cloud access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API is free; requires purchasing Hue hardware (Bridge ~$60, bulbs ~$15-60 each)
pricing.paid_tiers: No API pricing; hardware is the cost
pricing.notes: Developer registration is free. API usage is unlimited for local access. Remote API requires registering an app on developer portal.

performance.rate_limits: Local: ~30 messages/second to bridge; 10 commands/second per group recommended

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLIP v2 API has excellent documentation with type schemas. Error responses follow structured JSON format with error code and description. Server-Sent Events for real-time state changes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Light and group state changes are idempotent — setting a light to brightness 100 twice results in brightness 100. PUT operations are fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Button press required on physical Hue Bridge during initial API key setup
Rate limit: 30 messages/sec total to bridge, 10/sec per group — exceeding causes drops
CLIP v2 uses self-signed TLS certificate from bridge — must trust or bypass certificate validation
Bridge operates on local LAN only (unless using remote API with additional cloud latency)
Entertainment API (real-time RGB sync) uses UDP and requires different setup

security_score_components.security_notes: CLIP v2 uses TLS with bridge-issued certificate. Physical button press requirement prevents remote unauthorized access. Local-only by default (good network isolation). Remote API adds Signify cloud layer.
